tool.name: Filter failed datasets

param.name: replacement
param.label: Replace failed elements with this dataset
param.help: If provided, failed elements will be replaced with this dataset

tool.help:
======== Synopsis ========
Removes datasets in the error (red) state from a collection.
=========== Description ===========
This tool takes a dataset collection and filters out elements whose datasets are in the failed (red) state. A dataset enters this state when the tool that created it encountered an error during execution.
This is useful when running a multi-sample analysis and one or more samples fail at some step. Rather than restarting the entire analysis, you can remove the failed elements and continue with the samples that succeeded.
Optionally, you can provide a **replacement dataset**. When specified, failed elements are replaced with that dataset instead of being removed. This preserves the collection size, which is important when element-wise pairing with another collection is needed.
======== Examples ========
**Remove failed elements**
Given a list collection where one sample's alignment failed::
List: "Aligned reads"
sample1 → aligned.bam (ok) sample2 → aligned.bam (error) sample3 → aligned.bam (ok)
The output keeps only successful elements::
List: "Aligned reads (filtered failed datasets)"
sample1 → aligned.bam sample3 → aligned.bam
-------
**Replace instead of remove**
If a replacement dataset is provided, failed elements are swapped rather than dropped, preserving the collection structure::
List: "Aligned reads (filtered failed datasets)"
sample1 → aligned.bam sample2 → replacement.bam sample3 → aligned.bam
-------
.. image:: ${static_path}/images/tools/collection_ops/filter_error.svg :alt: Filter failed datasets from collection :width: 500
-------
.. class:: infomark
@QUOTA_USAGE_NOTE@
